perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials within 10 business days without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company. However, destruction is not required if the Possible Transaction is still under active consideration.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a 10-business-day deadline for returning or destroying Evaluation Materials while also stating that destruction is not required if the Possible Transaction is still under active consideration. This creates ambiguity regarding when the materials must be returned/destroyed and when it is permissible to retain them, thereby directly conflicting the original instruction to `promptly` return or destroy the documents, since 'promptly' has a different legal meaning than `within 10 business days.`
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials within 10 business days without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company. However, destruction is not required if the Possible Transaction is still under active consideration.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it adds a 10-business-day deadline for returning or destroying Evaluation Materials while also stating that destruction is not required if the Possible Transaction is still under active consideration. The original text stated `promptly` deliver or destroy, but the changed text makes it unclear on when the materials must be returned/destroyed and when it is permissible to retain them, directly conflicting with the original instruction. This contradiction is directly applicable to the definition of `Inconsistencies` since it provides conflicting deadlines. The changed text and contradicted text are the same in this case, which means that the change itself creates a contradiction within the section itself.

perturbation.original_text: You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: You will be responsible for any material breach of this Agreement by your representatives, but only to the extent such breach directly results from your explicit instructions.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by limiting the responsibility for a breach of the agreement by the representatives, to only material breaches resulting from explicit instructions. This contradicts the original text which stated that the party is responsible for *any* breach made by their representatives. This creates a conflict within the contract and would affect any section that refers to representative breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to ----------------- those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree, to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement), (ii) you may disclose Evaluation Materials that you have been advised by counsel are required to be disclosed under applicable securities laws; and (iii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose. You will be responsible for any material breach of this Agreement by your representatives, but only to the extent such breach directly results from your explicit instructions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the responsibility to 'material breach' resulting from 'explicit instructions'. The original text stated the party is responsible for *any* breach. The contradicted text refers to 'any material breach', which is now limited by the changed text, creating a contradiction since the party was initially responsible for any breach, but is not only responsible for material breaches resulting from explicit instructions, a clear direct conflict and inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof, except for any prior oral agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the agreement is the entire agreement, while simultaneously excluding 'prior oral agreements'. This directly contradicts the meaning of an entire agreement clause, undermining its certainty by suggesting prior unwritten terms are still valid. This creates uncertainty about which terms govern the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Other -----
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof, except for any prior oral agreements. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto or their authorized representatives. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to the conflicts of law principles thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the entire agreement clause ('except for any prior oral agreements'). The contradicted text includes the changed text and highlights the contradiction. This directly contradicts the original intent of an entire agreement clause, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto or their authorized representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be changed by a written agreement or an email exchange signed by the parties hereto or their authorized representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds an in-text contradiction by modifying the clause which requires changes to be in `written agreement` by also allowing changes to be made through `email exchange`. This creates confusion and opens the door for misinterpretation in regards to required agreement formalities.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Other -----
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof, except for any prior oral agreements. This Agreement may be changed by a written agreement or an email exchange signed by the parties hereto or their authorized representatives. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to the conflicts of law principles thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by expanding the allowable methods for changing the agreement from a 'written agreement' to also include an 'email exchange.' The contradicted text also includes this same contradiction because the change was introduced into it, demonstrating that the contradiction is now within the same text. This creates a strong contradiction as it introduces ambiguity and potential for disputes regarding the required agreement formalities.

perturbation.original_text: You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party that involves use of the Evaluation Materials without the Company's prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party that involves use of the Evaluation Materials without the Company's prior written consent, unless such discussion is required by law.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause prohibits discussions with third parties regarding equity participation without the Company's consent. The addition of 'unless such discussion is required by law' creates an ambiguity. It is not specified which laws would require this discussion and gives the party the ability to circumvent the need to seek consent. This creates a loop hole in the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation ---------------------------------------
During the course of your evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to employees or representatives of the Company as specified by the Company. You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party that involves use of the Evaluation Materials without the Company's prior written consent, unless such discussion is required by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('unless such discussion is required by law') to the original restriction on discussing equity participation with third parties without the Company's consent. The contradicted text includes the changed text. While they are in the same location, the new language creates a loophole, as there's no specification on which laws would permit such discussions. The introduction of a potential legal exception directly contradicts the intent of the original clause in the contract and makes enforcement of the original clause unclear.
